pattern: Heights are tiny, so bucket rectangle lengths by height and, for each point, binary-search how many lengths reach the point's x across every qualifying height.
hints: A rectangle contains a point exactly when its length is at least the point's x and its height is at least the point's y.
The height values are bounded by a small constant, so group the rectangle lengths into a bucket per height.
Sort each height bucket once; for a point, sum over heights at least its y the count of lengths at least its x, found by binary search.

edge_inputs.description: nested rectangles, point at origin-ish corner
point exactly on an edge is contained
tall but short rectangle misses a far-right point

stress.description: many rectangles and points with heights from a small range and lengths spread wide
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nr = rng.randint(1, min(size, 2000))
    np_ = rng.randint(1, min(size, 2000))
    seen_r = set()
    rectangles = []
    while len(rectangles) < nr:
        l = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
        h = rng.randint(1, 100)
        if (l, h) in seen_r:
            continue
        seen_r.add((l, h))
        rectangles.append([l, h])
    seen_p = set()
    points = []
    while len(points) < np_:
        x = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
        y = rng.randint(1, 100)
        if (x, y) in seen_p:
            continue
        seen_p.add((x, y))
        points.append([x, y])
    return [rectangles, points]
stress.note: rectangles and points are de-duplicated as the constraints require uniqueness
